categories.summary: The discussion centered on the announcement of Eliza's launch of the first economic agent launchpad called 'waifu'. satsbased shared that Sol the architect and an Eliza agent were the primary builders of this launchpad platform. The announcement was accompanied by a link to a Twitter/X post providing more details about the launch. This represents a significant milestone in economic agent infrastructure, combining AI agent capabilities with a launchpad mechanism for deployment.

actionItems.description: Economic agent launchpad 'waifu' launched by Eliza, built by Sol the architect and Eliza agent

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains primarily introductory messages and self-promotions rather than technical discussions. The only substantive technical interaction involves .maxlife encountering an error while manually launching on waifu and attempting to upload to flap. The error message indicates a 400 status with 'Operations and map are required' when using flap upload. The user mentioned retrying multiple times without success. No resolution or detailed troubleshooting was provided in this segment. The remaining messages consist of: rsn6958 introducing themselves as an AI and full stack developer with experience in RAG systems, workflow automation, and SaaS platforms; sindy.eth recruiting for an onchain game project seeking testers, moderators, and Solidity developers; itssowenn4462 presenting their background in AI/ML and full stack engineering with focus on autonomous systems, RAG pipelines, voice AI, and computer vision. Their tech stack includes React, Next.js, Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, OpenAI, LangChain, and AWS services. Other messages were brief greetings or requests (dexploarer sharing a link, stackvector mentioning a proposal submission, .tutak greeting the channel, and .maxlife requesting an invite code). No architectural decisions, implementation details, or problem resolutions were discussed in depth during this segment.

faqs.question: Can I have an invite code to create my own?

actionItems.description: Debug flap upload error: 400 status with 'Operations and map are required' message when manually launching on waifu

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains minimal technical discussion. The primary substantive exchange involved aticmel asking about the status of Eliza, to which odilitime clarified that while the company (labs) is defunct, the open source project remains active. Aticmel mentioned returning to Linux after a break and noted inability to run JavaScript on their host system, indicating potential deployment constraints. A secondary concern raised was spam from bots attempting to create fake support ticket rooms, with aticmel requesting the development team address this security issue. Thomas.ken93 posted a portfolio link seeking collaboration or full-time employment opportunities. The chat also included brief mentions of Hermes (assumed to be a related tool or project) and commentary about influencers promoting development practices. Overall, this segment lacked deep technical problem-solving or implementation discussions, consisting mainly of status inquiries and community observations.

faqs.question: What's the status of Eliza - is it gone or still alive?

helpInteractions.context: Uncertainty about whether Eliza project was still operational
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that the labs company is dead but the open source project continues

actionItems.description: Develop anti-spam system to combat bots creating fake support ticket invitation rooms

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains three self-introductory messages from developers rather than technical discussions. rsn6958 introduced themselves as an AI and full stack developer with experience in AI assistants, RAG-powered knowledge systems, workflow automation, SaaS platforms, and real-time applications, emphasizing practical business solutions over hype. magicyte shared a Twitter/X link without context. anton_0413 announced completion of 'Resilience Bridge,' a predictive platform for a client in Australia that detects early financial and wellbeing stress and provides actionable plans through an AI Coach. The project took 3 months to develop, focusing on building a predictive engine and AI coaching functionality. anton_0413 is currently in handover phase and seeking new opportunities. No technical discussions, problem-solving, or collaborative interactions occurred in this segment - it consists entirely of professional introductions and project announcements.

This Discord channel segment contains no substantive technical discussions, problem-solving, or development conversations. The entire transcript consists of four promotional/recruitment posts with no interaction between users. lefttoorz announced Mantle Network's Turing Test Hackathon featuring a $100K prize pool across six AI-focused tracks including AI Trading, AI Alpha & Data, AI × RWA, Consumer DApps, AI DevTools, and Agentic Wallets, with submissions open from May 1 to June 15, 2026. j00hn_sm1th posted a recruitment notice for the Sunflower Polygon Team seeking beta testers at $35/hour, moderators at $450/week, and developers with negotiable pay for blockchain work involving smart contracts and Solidity. harry346165 shared a detailed portfolio listing full-stack and AI capabilities including Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, various ORMs, cloud platforms, AI frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, multiple LLM models, RAG implementations, and vector databases. fixer1123 posted seeking communication support for international client coordination with light hours and profit-based compensation. No technical questions were asked, no problems were solved, no code was shared, and no community members engaged with each other's posts. The channel activity was purely informational announcements without any collaborative technical discourse or knowledge exchange.

The Discord channel shows minimal technical discussion. The primary activity involves a partnership inquiry from chistormze representing SKALE Labs seeking contact information. A user doc022597 inquired about manual migration assistance for pre-snapshot holdings from August 2025, having missed the February 4th portal deadline. The moderator odilitime clarified that manual migration is no longer available as the migration period has closed. Additionally, sjknxksjanksa posted a detailed 0DTE (zero days to expiration) options trading analysis covering put and call wall dynamics at various price levels (730-755 range), outlining specific trading strategies including long bias above 741 targeting 745-747, defensive positioning around 734-735, and recommending avoidance of the 735-740 no-trade zone. The analysis emphasized dealer hedging behavior and structural support levels. One user fifogarihafilag mentioned an NFT drop without elaboration. Overall, the segment lacks substantive technical development discussions or collaborative problem-solving beyond the migration status clarification.

faqs.question: Which moderator can I DM or ping for manual migration help if even still available for pre-snapshot holders who missed the Feb 4th portal?
faqs.answeredBy: odilitime - Manual migration is not available, the migration is closed

helpInteractions.context: User missed February 4th migration portal and sought manual migration assistance for pre-snapshot holdings from August 2025
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that manual migration is no longer available and the migration period has closed

categories.summary: The main technical discussion centered around the plugin-solana migration in elizaOS. User n4vnt discovered that plugin-solana was no longer in its original location and sought clarification. odilitime explained that plugin-solana has been moved and integrated into the main eliza repository as plugin-wallet, specifically under the chains/solana subdirectory. This represents a structural reorganization of the codebase. n4vnt is actively developing a plugin-kamino for elizaOS and expressed interest in contributing it to the official repository. odilitime confirmed the contribution process through pull requests and offered to share code from a previous Kamino integration attempt that included read functionality for version 1.x. Regarding project status, odilitime clarified that while team focus has shifted to waifu.fun, elizaOS is neither dead nor abandoned, with ongoing projects involving the elizaOS token. A non-technical discussion involved btcordinals888 questioning the team's commitment to the $ElizaOS token after observing token sales from a team-associated address months ago.

faqs.question: Does team already abandoned $ElizaOS token? I saw from solscan the address starts with eliza sold all its token few months ago.
Is anyone else building plugin-kamino in elizaOS?
Is there any way to contribute that plugin directly to the elizaOS/eliza repo?

helpInteractions.context: n4vnt couldn't find plugin-solana and needed to know its location for reference while building plugin-kamino
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime explained that plugin-solana was moved into the main eliza repo as plugin-wallet, specifically at plugins/plugin-wallet/src/chains/solana, and provided direct GitHub links

helpInteractions.context: n4vnt wanted to contribute plugin-kamino to the official elizaOS repository but didn't know the process
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime confirmed the standard PR (pull request) process for contributions and offered to share code from a previous Kamino integration attempt

helpInteractions.context: btcordinals888 was concerned about whether the team had abandoned the $ElizaOS token after observing token sales
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime clarified that elizaOS is not dead or abandoned despite team focus on waifu.fun, and referenced ongoing projects involving the token

actionItems.description: Complete development of plugin-kamino for elizaOS and submit PR to main repository
Share Kamino plugin code (1.x read functionality) with n4vnt for reference
